sections.headline: GCC markets open mixed amid oil price volatility
sections.summary: GCC stock markets opened mixed on Thursday, with Saudi Arabia's Tadawul down 0.3% and Dubai's DFM up 0.5%, as Brent crude hovered near $78 per barrel.
sections.insight: Divergent performance reflects ongoing uncertainty in global oil markets, but regional fundamentals remain supported by strong fiscal buffers and diversification efforts.

sections.headline: Saudi non-oil PMI rises to 58.2 in April
sections.summary: Saudi Arabia's non-oil Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) rose to 58.2 in April from 57.8 in March, indicating robust expansion in the non-oil private sector.
sections.insight: Sustained PMI readings above 55 underscore the resilience of Saudi non-oil activity, driven by strong domestic demand and government spending under Vision 2030.

sections.headline: UAE GDP growth forecast at 4.1% for 2026
sections.summary: The UAE's GDP is projected to grow 4.1% in 2026, according to the latest IMF forecast, supported by tourism, real estate, and financial services.
sections.insight: The UAE's diversified economy continues to outperform regional peers, with non-oil sectors contributing over 70% of GDP growth this year.

sections.headline: Regional fintech funding hits $1.2bn in Q1 2026
sections.summary: Fintech startups in the GCC raised $1.2 billion in Q1 2026, a 35% increase year-on-year, led by Saudi Arabia and the UAE.
sections.insight: The surge in fintech investment reflects growing digital adoption and supportive regulatory frameworks, positioning the GCC as a global fintech hub.

sections.headline: Saudi Aramco maintains dividend at $31bn for Q1
sections.summary: Saudi Aramco reported Q1 2026 net income of $27.5 billion and maintained its dividend at $31 billion, despite lower oil production.
sections.insight: Aramco's steady dividend underscores its cash flow resilience and commitment to shareholder returns, even as OPEC+ output cuts persist.

sections.headline: Dubai real estate transactions surge 22% in April
sections.summary: Dubai recorded 12,500 real estate transactions in April 2026, up 22% year-on-year, with total value exceeding AED 38 billion.
sections.insight: Continued strong demand from foreign investors and high-net-worth individuals is driving Dubai's property market, supported by visa reforms and economic stability.

sections.headline: Qatar LNG exports rise 8% in Q1 2026
sections.summary: Qatar's liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports increased 8% year-on-year in Q1 2026, reaching 21 million tonnes, as global demand for cleaner energy grows.
sections.insight: Qatar's expansion of LNG capacity positions it to capture a larger share of the global gas market, especially as Europe diversifies away from Russian supplies.
